Write a follow up noting TO&C is nowhere to be seen
User1: Hey, have you noticed that The Owl & Co. isn’t on the new extended CBBC schedule?

User2: You’re right, I just looked through the entire lineup and it’s nowhere to be seen. I wonder why they decided to leave it out.

User1: It’s a shame, I really enjoyed that show. Hopefully, they bring it back or replace it with something equally as entertaining.

User2: Yeah, hopefully they have some new exciting shows lined up for the evening time slot. I’ll miss The Owl & Co. but I’m sure there will be plenty of other great shows to watch. Write a forum convo about what’s most popular on CBBC right now (in this scenario), what isn’t doing well and what’s basically surviving
User1: So what do you think is currently the most popular show on CBBC?

User2: I would have to say that The Next Step seems to be really popular at the moment. It has a big fan base and always gets a lot of views.

User1: Yeah, I agree. It’s definitely a hit with the viewers. But what about shows that aren’t doing so well?

User2: I think Remotely Funny hasn’t been getting as much attention as some of the other shows. It’s a fun show, but it just doesn’t seem to be catching on with the audience.

User1: And what about the shows that are just surviving on the schedule?

User2: I’d say Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed is one of those shows that’s just comfortably hanging in there. It’s not the most popular, but it has its loyal viewers who tune in regularly.

User1: It’s interesting to see how different shows are faring on the channel. I wonder if CBBC will make any changes based on the popularity of certain shows. Write a forum convo about the early morning only shows, and one of them is doing pretty good for a forgotten cartoon that only airs first thing
User1: Have you noticed how well Skunk Fu is doing as one of the early morning only shows on CBBC?

User2: Yes, it’s actually surprising how popular it has become considering it only airs first thing in the morning. It seems to have gained a new following among viewers.

User1: That’s great to hear! It’s always nice to see a forgotten cartoon do well and find a new audience. I hope CBBC continues to showcase these early morning gems. Write a Wikipedia page set in May 2016 with current programming (with the year it originally debuted on the channel, and if a show has left for a period, then the year it returned will appear next to it, by the way its still part of this scenario)
CBBC (Children’s BBC) is a British children’s television channel owned and operated by the BBC. As of May 2016, the channel offers a variety of popular programming for children of all ages.

Current programming on CBBC:

- Skunk Fu (2007-2010, January 02 2016-)
- Arthur (2002)
- Danger Mouse (2015)
- The Next Step (2013)
- Operation Ouch! (2015)
- Horrible Histories (2009)
- Blue Peter (1959)
- Odd Squad (2014)
- Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed
- Scream Street
- So Awkward
- Shaun the Sheep (2007)
- Class Dismissed
- Jamie Johnson
- Remotely Funny
- Lost and Found
- The Dumping Ground
- The Zoo
- The Playlist
- Now You See It
- Junior Vets
- Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
- Deadly Art
- The Deep (March 01 2016-)
- Hetty Feather
- Millie Inbetween

One of the standout shows that has become a surprise success is Skunk Fu, which originally debuted on the channel in 2007. Despite being a forgotten cartoon that only airs in the early morning, it has managed to attract a new audience and has become a popular show among viewers.

Overall, CBBC continues to offer a diverse range of programming that caters to the interests and preferences of its young audience. Viewers can expect entertaining and educational shows that keep them engaged and coming back for more. Write a schedule change on May 1 which sees some suprises in the evening lineup

Write a follow up where a user notices a new 2016 Upfront trailer running on the channel, the logos of 10 shows are here, also P&F is coming soon, but we are super SUPRISED to see one show mentioned in the trailer, considering it hasn't aired new eps in YEARS

User1: Did anyone else catch the new 2016 Upfront trailer running on CBBC? They showcased logos of 10 current shows, and there's a hint that Phineas and Ferb (P&F) is coming soon to the channel. But one show really surprised me.

User2: Wait, P&F is coming to CBBC? That's amazing news! But now you've got me curious. Which show surprised you in the trailer?

User1: Okay, hold onto your hats, because they included a logo for "Adventures of Tintin" in the trailer. Yes, that "Adventures of Tintin"! Considering it hasn't aired new episodes in YEARS, I was totally taken aback.

User2: "Adventures of Tintin"?! No way, that's a blast from the past! I can't believe they're bringing it back. Did the trailer say anything about new episodes, or are they just rerunning the classic ones?

User1: The trailer didn’t specify, unfortunately. All it showed was the logo among the other shows airing this year. It looks like we'll have to keep our eyes peeled for more news on this. If they are new episodes, that would be absolutely incredible.

User2: Seriously, this is the best news I've heard all day! "Adventures of Tintin" used to be my favorite show. Whether it's reruns or new episodes, I'm just excited it’s getting some love again. Thanks for the heads up, I’ll definitely be watching out for more info!
Write a follow up with the 10 shows shown in the trailer

User1: So, I did some digging and found out which 10 shows were featured in the CBBC Upfront 2016 trailer. Apart from the jaw-dropping inclusion of "Adventures of Tintin", here’s what else is on the roster:

1. **The Next Step** – This one's been a staple for dance drama lovers. Really no surprise there.
2. **Horrible Histories** – Always a hit. Their unique take on history is both educational and hilarious.
3. **Operation Ouch!** – With the amount of love this show gets for its fun and fascinating look at the human body and medicine, it’s a shoo-in.
4. **Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed** – The misadventures continue. This show has a solid fan base.
5. **Blue Peter** – It’s historic at this point. No CBBC lineup would feel right without it.
6. **Hetty Feather** – The dramatic adventures based on the Jacqueline Wilson book have been a hit.
7. **Shaun the Sheep** – Aardman’s genius at work. It’s timeless and appeals to pretty much everyone.
8. **So Awkward** – That awkward teenage life resonates well, it seems!
9. **The Deep** - This one's for the adventure seekers who love the mysteries of the deep sea.
10. **Adventures of Tintin** – As we’ve already discussed. It’s making a return, and it’s the biggest surprise of them all!

And, of course, they teased that **Phineas and Ferb** is coming soon, which is super exciting. Can't wait to see how they integrate it into the current lineup. It looks like CBBC is going all out this year!

User2: Wow, that's an impressive lineup! CBBC is not holding back. "The Adventures of Tintin" coming back is definitely the highlight for me, but I'm also stoked for "Phineas and Ferb." Looks like we're going to be spoilt for choice. Thanks for the list!
Write a follow up with the June 21 schedule with P&F added

5:45pm - **Shaun the Sheep**

User2: Phineas and Ferb three times in one day? CBBC is really showing it some love! This is going to be excellent, especially in the summer when we can all reminisce about trying to make every day the best day ever, just like Phineas and Ferb.

User1: Absolutely! And the rest of the lineup is solid too. CBBC has done a great job at mixing new shows with beloved classics. Can't wait for June 21st; it's going to be a fantastic day for tuning in.

User1: Exactly! It looks like CBBC is really shaking things up and maybe trying to bring more family viewing into the evening schedule. **Operation Ouch!** has a broad appeal, so it makes sense. Plus, with such a diverse lineup leading up to it, the channel is setting a nice pace for viewers throughout the day.

User2: True, it's a smart move. Gonna be interesting to see how this plays out and if other shows might get similar shifts in the future based on their popularity. Exciting times ahead for CBBC viewers!
Write a follow up wondering where SF went

User1: Just noticed something missing from the CBBC schedule for June 21st. Where did Shaun the Sheep go? It's usually a staple in the morning lineup, but I can't find it in the evening slots either.

User2: That's a good catch. Shaun the Sheep has been a beloved show on CBBC for years. It's odd not seeing it on the schedule at all, especially when they're extending programming to 9pm.

User1: Yeah, exactly. It's one of those shows that's perfect for winding down in the evening or starting your day with a laugh in the morning. I wonder why it would be left off the schedule entirely. Maybe it's just for June 21st?

User2: Could be. There might be a special event or marathon of another show that day which caused a few regulars like Shaun the Sheep to be temporarily bumped. Or perhaps they're shifting around the programming to accommodate new shows or test new time slots for others.

User1: That makes sense. I hope it's just a one-day thing. Shaun the Sheep has a way of bringing a smile to people's faces, no matter what age. Would be a shame to lose that from the daily lineup.

User2: Agreed. Let's keep an eye on the schedule for the following days. Hopefully, Shaun the Sheep makes its return soon. CBBC wouldn't be the same without it.
Writr a follow up saying Shaun is fine, its Skunk Fu that's gone,

User1: My mistake, everyone! I got my wires crossed when looking at the schedule. Shaun the Sheep is actually fine and still in its usual slot. It's Skunk Fu that's disappeared from the lineup.

User2: Oh, that clears things up about Shaun. But it's a shame about Skunk Fu. That show had a unique charm to it, even if it wasn't as popular as some of the other titles.

User1: Yeah, Skunk Fu had its niche audience for sure. It's always tough to see a show go, especially one that's been part of the early morning routine. It had a good run, but I guess the programming team must have their reasons for the change.

User2: True, everything comes to an end at some point. And with the channel expanding its evening slots, it's only natural some shows would be shuffled around or phased out to make room for new content. Hopefully, Skunk Fu fans will find something else in the lineup to enjoy.

User1: Absolutely. With Phineas and Ferb joining the schedule and other shows moving around, there's still lots to look forward to. CBBC knows how to keep their programming fresh and engaging. Maybe we'll even see Skunk Fu return in some form in the future. Never say never with beloved shows!
Write a email from a parent asking where it went, and CBBC reply that its not "returning yet" and here's 5 other shows he might like (P&F is mentioned first)

Subject: Inquiry About Skunk Fu's Disappearance from CBBC Schedule

Dear CBBC,

I hope this message finds you well. As a parent overseeing the content my children engage with, I've come to appreciate the variety and quality of shows offered on your channel. However, I've recently noticed the absence of "Skunk Fu" from your schedule, a show that my children, especially the youngest, have grown quite fond of. It felt like a safe and entertaining choice for them, encouraging creativity and offering valuable lessons through its storytelling.

Could you please provide information regarding the removal of "Skunk Fu" from your programming? Will it be returning any time soon, or has it been discontinued permanently? If so, my child would be quite disappointed, and I would appreciate any recommendations for similar content available on CBBC that could serve as suitable alternatives.

Thank you for your time and assistance. Looking forward to your response.

Warm regards,

[Your Name]

---

Dear [Your Name],

Thank you for reaching out to us at CBBC and for your kind words about our programming. We understand that changes to our schedule can be disappointing, especially when it involves a show as enjoyed as "Skunk Fu." We strive to offer a diverse and dynamic range of programming that can cater to the varied interests of our young audiences.

To clarify, "Skunk Fu" has been removed from our current broadcasting schedule and, as of now, there are no immediate plans for its return. We regularly review our lineup to keep it fresh and engaging, thus making way for new content and giving other shows a platform. While we know this might be sad news, we’re eager to suggest a few alternatives that we believe might equally capture your child's imagination and interest:

1. **Phineas and Ferb** – An inventive and amusing show that encourages creativity and problem-solving, with plenty of humor and adventure to keep the whole family entertained.

2. **Shaun the Sheep** – A delightful and cleverly crafted series that, much like "Skunk Fu," delivers silent comedy through its charming characters and storytelling.

3. **Danger Mouse** – Full of action and humor, this animated series offers exciting adventures that can captivate fans of animated heroics.

4. **Horrible Histories** – A fun way to learn about history with a twist, presenting historical facts in a humorous and accessible way for children.

5. **Blue Peter** – The longest-running children’s TV show in the world that includes a variety of segments ranging from arts and crafts to environmental projects and competitions, encouraging participation and creativity.

We hope these suggestions provide your family with new favorites to enjoy together. We greatly value feedback from our viewers, and your comments have been noted for consideration in our future programming decisions.

Thank you once again for your support of CBBC. Please don’t hesitate to reach out if you have further questions or need additional recommendations.

Warmest wishes,

The CBBC Team
Write a follow up where bbc.co.uk/cbbc/skunkfu even redirects

User1: Hey fellow parents, just wanted to share a bit of an update regarding "Skunk Fu" on CBBC. After noticing it was missing from the schedule and following up with CBBC directly, I tried to do a bit more digging on my own. I thought maybe there could be some online episodes or activities related to "Skunk Fu" on the CBBC website, as they sometimes host content there even for shows not currently airing. However, it seems that even attempting to access bbc.co.uk/cbbc/skunkfu now redirects to the CBBC homepage with no direct mention of "Skunk Fu" at all.

It looks like CBBC has decided to fully phase out "Skunk Fu" from its current offerings, at least for the time being. While the response from CBBC did suggest some fantastic alternative shows (with "Phineas and Ferb" at the forefront, which my kids are already loving), it's a bit sad to see such finality in the removal of "Skunk Fu" from their digital presence as well.

Has anyone else noticed this or found any other shows that filled the gap for their "Skunk Fu"-loving kids? I'm always looking for recommendations that encourage creativity and offer valuable lessons like "Skunk Fu" did. Would love to hear your thoughts and suggestions.

Best, [Your Name]
Write a convo in November 2016 about if it's back then

User1: Hey everyone, has anyone noticed if "Skunk Fu" has made a comeback to the CBBC lineup yet? It's been a few months since they took it off, and November's schedule is out. 

User2: Just checked the latest schedule for November 2016, and unfortunately, "Skunk Fu" is still not on it. Looks like CBBC hasn't brought it back yet. My kids were hoping it would return too. 

User3: Yeah, it's a shame. "Skunk Fu" had a unique charm. We've been filling the gap with "Phineas and Ferb" and a few others recommended by CBBC, but my youngest still asks about it. 

User4: I've been keeping an eye on the CBBC site and haven’t seen any updates about "Skunk Fu" either. It seems like they're focusing on the newer shows for now. 

User5: On a positive note, the new shows CBBC has added are pretty good, and it’s nice to see them refreshing the lineup. But I do hope they consider bringing back some of the old favorites like "Skunk Fu" at some point. 

User1: Agreed, the new lineup has been great, and the kids have found new favorites, but having classics like "Skunk Fu" would add nice variety. Maybe we should start a little feedback campaign to CBBC, expressing our interest in seeing it return? 

User2: That's a fantastic idea, User1! A feedback campaign might just show CBBC there's still a considerable audience for "Skunk Fu." Count me in. 

User3: Count us in too! It would be wonderful to have "Skunk Fu" back for the younger siblings who missed it the first time around. 

User4: Definitely worth a shot. Let's organize and see if we can make a difference. Who knows, maybe by next year, we might see it back on the schedule! 

User1: Great, let's do this! I'll draft up a petition and share it here for everyone to sign. Here’s hoping our collective voices can bring some classic fun back to CBBC.
Write a convo where Iplayer has 14 P&F eps recommended, and list them, also the 2nd listed is the one where they are busted

User1: Hey, fellow parents! Have you seen the latest on BBC iPlayer? They've got 14 episodes of Phineas and Ferb recommended right now. My kids are thrilled!

User2: Really? That's fantastic news! P&F is a favorite in our household. Do you have the list of episodes they've featured? My kids would love to plan a weekend marathon.

User1: Sure do! Here's the breakdown of the episodes available:

1. "Rollercoaster" - The classic that started it all, where Phineas and Ferb build their first rollercoaster in the backyard.
2. "At Last" - **The episode where they are finally busted!** It's a rollercoaster ride of emotions.
3. "The Fast and the Phineas" - Car racing like you've never seen it, Phineas and Ferb style.
4. "Lawn Gnome Beach Party of Terror" - The boys bring the beach to their backyard, complete with a surfing contest.
5. "S'Winter" - Who says you can't have snow in summer? An episode full of cool inventions.
6. "Raging Bully" - Phineas enters a thumb-wrestling competition with Buford.
7. "Lights, Candace, Action!" - Candace stars in a movie directed by the boys, based on her favorite childhood book.
8. "Jerk De Soleil" - The boys create their own circus to help cheer up Isabella.
9. "Toy to the World" - Phineas and Ferb design a toy so fun it catches the attention of a major toy company.
10. "One Good Scare Ought to Do It!" - A haunted house is the boys' solution to help Isabella get over her hiccups.
11. "The Flying Fishmonger" - Grandpa Fletcher's old daredevil act gets a Phineas and Ferb makeover.
12. "Ain't No Kiddie Ride" - Those coin-operated rides in front of the grocery store? The boys take them to the next level.
13. "The Ballad of Badbeard" - Candace believes she's found a map to pirate treasure.
14. "Greece Lightning" - A chariot race around Danville brings ancient Greece to modern life.

User3: Wow, that's a fantastic selection! “At Last” is going to be such a hit here. The anticipation of them getting busted has always been a hilarious part of the show.

User4: Absolutely! I'm most excited about the "Greece Lightning" episode. I love how they integrate educational elements with fun. Makes for great conversations with the kids afterward.

User5: Thanks for sharing! It looks like it's going to be a Phineas and Ferb-filled weekend for us. BBC iPlayer really nailed it with this selection.

User1: No problem! Glad to share the fun. It’s a great mix of episodes that captures the essence of what makes Phineas and Ferb so special. Enjoy the marathon, everyone!
